title: Tender Filipino Beans Ginataang Bilo Bilo
description: A traditional Filipino dessert featuring tender beans and pillowy bilo bilo in a rich coconut milk broth, perfect for a post-workout snack or dessert. This shojin-friendly dish is easy to make and delicious to eat.
instructions: Combine coconut milk, water, pandan leaves, sugar, and salt in a large pot and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to medium and simmer for 10 minutes or until the mixture has thickened slightly.
Add tapioca pearls and cook for an additional 5 minutes or until they are translucent and cooked through.
Add cooked azuki beans to the pot and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes or until the beans are heated through.
Remove pandan leaves from the pot and discard.
Serve the ginataang bilo bilo hot or at room temperature.
Garnish with additional pandan leaves if desired.
